disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Palpitations and marked heart racing on becoming upright, settling when lying flat
trigger_question_examples: Tell me about the racing heart — when does it happen?
Does anything bring the palpitations on or settle them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The second I sit up or stand, my heart just takes off and pounds. If I lie back down flat it settles again after a minute or two.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent pre-syncope with visual greying on standing, relieved by lying down; no completed syncope this episode
trigger_question_examples: What happens when you stand up — do you feel dizzy or faint?
Have you actually passed out, or just felt like you might?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When I stand my vision greys out and I feel like I'm about to black out, so I have to get down fast. I haven't actually fully passed out this time — I've caught it each time by lying down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negatives screening serious mimics — no melena or haematemesis, no chest pain at rest, tachycardia is positional and resolves supine rather than fixed
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any black tarry stools, or vomiting blood?
Do you get the chest racing when you're just lying still, or only when upright?
Any chest pain, or does the fast heart rate stay fast even lying down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No black or bloody stools and I haven't vomited any blood. There's no chest pain, and when I lie flat the racing settles right down — it's really only when I'm upright.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really thought about that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome with easily subluxing joints and a history of identical recurrent flares
trigger_question_examples: Have you had episodes like this before?
Can you tell me about your Ehlers-Danlos and how it usually affects you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I've had my Ehlers-Danlos for years — my joints are super loose and pop out easily. I get flares where the racing heart, dizziness and joint pain all blow up together like this. This one's just worse than usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone with high fall risk from combined orthostatic intolerance and joint instability; currently bedbound and crawling to toilet
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone at home with you at the moment?
How are you managing to move around — is falling a worry?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's just me at home. I'm too dizzy to walk so I've been sliding along the floor to the toilet, and I've put my mattress on the floor because I keep nearly fainting. Falling is a real worry.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Emergency screen currently negative — no completed faint with head strike, no locked/deformed joint dislocation, no breathing difficulty
trigger_question_examples: Have you fainted and hit your head, or is any joint currently out and stuck or misshapen?
Are you having any trouble breathing, or is a limb pale, cold or numb?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — I haven't actually collapsed or hit my head, nothing's dislocated and stuck right now, and my breathing is fine. It's the racing, dizziness and aching that are the problem.
